div.p: Impact of Ping Timeout and Indirect Probe Fanout on Detection Accuracy

g.text: False Positive Rate vs Packet Loss Rate

div.p: At 5% loss: ~5% FP rate Single point of failure
At 5% loss: ~0.25% FP rate Below threshold ✓
At 5% loss: ~0.0125% FP rate Recommended config ✓
At 5% loss: ~0.0003% FP rate Diminishing returns

g.text: Timeout | P99 RTT | FP Rate | Status

div.p: Rule of Thumb: ping_timeout ≥ 3 × P99_RTT Calibrate with CalibratePingTimeout() in swim/config.go at startup
Production (High Reliability) go Config{ PingTimeout: 500ms, IndirectFanout: 3, ProtocolPeriod: 1s, SuspicionMultiplier: 5, }
Development (Fast Detection) go Config{ PingTimeout: 200ms, IndirectFanout: 2, ProtocolPeriod: 500ms, SuspicionMultiplier: 3, }

g.text: Detection Time | 1 RTT | 2 RTT | 2 RTT | 2 RTT

div.p: Diminishing Returns Warning: Beyond k=3, FP rate improvement is negligible:

ul.li: k=3 → k=5: 20x better FP, but 1.5x more bandwidth
k=3 → k=7: 400x better FP, but 2x more bandwidth For most networks (1-5% loss), k=3 is optimal. Only use k>3 in extreme loss environments (>10%).

div.p: Test Verification: TestFalsePositiveRate() in swim_test.go Runs 1000 protocol periods at 5% packet loss Asserts: falsePositiveRate < 0.01 (1%)
False Positive Probability: P(false_positive) = P_loss^(k+1) Where:

ul.li: k+1 = direct + indirect attempts Example (5% loss, k=3): 0.05^4 = 0.00000625 ≈ 0.0006%
